title: grewia lactea
intro: Grewia lactea, also known as the milk berry or milk bush, is a plant species native to tropical Africa, characterized by its milky sap and edible fruits. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include antibacterial, antifungal, and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Grewia lactea include its potential to treat various conditions such as fever, diarrhea, and skin infections, as well as its ability to aid in wound healing. Herbal preparations of Grewia lactea can be made by infusing its leaves in hot water to create a tea, or by using its roots and bark to create a decoction for medicinal purposes.
